description: Engineered 6-residue thrombin recognition/cleavage site (LVPRGS) used to proteolytically remove affinity/solubility tags from recombinant fusion proteins; thrombin cleaves after the Arg (LVPR | GS), leaving a Gly-Ser N-terminal scar.

references.title: Single-step purification of polypeptides expressed in Escherichia coli as fusions with glutathione S-transferase

references.authors: Gallwitz M, Enoksson M, Thorpe M, Hellman L
references.title: The Extended Cleavage Specificity of Human Thrombin
An overview of enzymatic reagents for the removal of affinity tags

references.authors: Jenny RJ, Mann KG, Lundblad RL
references.title: A critical review of the methods for cleavage of fusion proteins with thrombin and factor Xa

references.authors: Liu D, Xu R, Dutta K, Cowburn D
references.title: N-terminal cysteinyl proteins can be prepared using thrombin cleavage
New vectors for high level expression of recombinant proteins in bacteria

label: thrombin_site
note: Engineered 6-aa thrombin (factor IIa) recognition/cleavage site Leu-Val-Pro-Arg-Gly-Ser. Thrombin hydrolyses the peptide bond after the Arg (LVPR | GS), releasing the downstream protein with an N-terminal Gly-Ser scar. Derived from the natural human factor VIII thrombin site LVPRGF by a Phe->Ser substitution that creates a BamHI (GGATCC) cloning site; the canonical site of the pGEX-2T GST-fusion expression system. Source-verified to U13850.1 misc_feature 918..935 at 100% identity over all 6 residues.

provenance.sequence_source: U13850.1 (pGEX-2T cloning vector, Pharmacia Biotech, deposited 1994-08-19), misc_feature 918..935 annotated 'encodes thrombin recognition site', nt CTGGTTCCGCGTGGATCC translates to LVPRGS, 100% over 6 aa; original design in Smith & Johnson 1988, Gene 67:31-40 (PMID 3047011, doi:10.1016/0378-1119(88)90005-4)

functional_claims.label: LVPRGS is an engineered thrombin (factor IIa) recognition/cleavage site used to proteolytically separate an affinity or solubility tag from a target protein in recombinant fusions

source.quote: Single-step purification of polypeptides expressed in Escherichia coli as fusions with glutathione S-transferase
source.section: title; thrombin cleavage of the GST-fusion linker

functional_claims.label: Thrombin cleaves the LVPRGS site after the Arg residue (LVPR | GS), releasing the downstream protein with an N-terminal Gly-Ser scar

source.quote: Thrombin cleaves after the Arg of LVPR|GS, leaving Gly-Ser residues on the N-terminus of the released protein
source.section: thrombin recognition site and resulting scar

functional_claims.label: LVPRGS was engineered from the natural human factor VIII thrombin cleavage site LVPRGF by a Phe->Ser substitution at P2' that creates a BamHI (GGATCC) restriction site for convenient cloning

source.quote: The thrombin site LVPRGS is derived from the factor VIII sequence LVPRGF, with the C-terminal Phe replaced by Ser to introduce a BamHI site

functional_claims.label: Thrombin's extended cleavage consensus is P2=Pro (dominant), P1=Arg (obligatory) and a small P1' residue, with P3'=Arg preferred; LVPRGS matches the core P2-Pro/P1-Arg/P1'-small determinants

source.quote: The Extended Cleavage Specificity of Human Thrombin
source.section: title; phage-display determination of P4-P4' specificity

functional_claims.label: Thrombin can cleave at cryptic/off-target sites within target proteins, so cleavage products require verification; this is a recognized limitation versus the more specific factor Xa and TEV proteases

source.quote: A critical review of the methods for cleavage of fusion proteins with thrombin and factor Xa

functional_claims.label: A Gly->Cys variant at P1' (LVPRC) lets thrombin expose an N-terminal cysteine on the released protein, enabling downstream native chemical ligation

source.quote: N-terminal cysteinyl proteins can be prepared using thrombin cleavage
source.section: title; LVPRC variant demonstrated with Csk and Abl kinase domains
